title: picea abies f. parsonsii
intro: Picea abies f. parsonsii, also known as Parsons' spruce, is a cultivar of the Norway spruce tree, identified by its distinctive conical shape and short, stiff branches. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been found to aid in wound healing and reduce swelling. The health benefits of Picea abies f. parsonsii include its ability to improve respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant properties, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. Picea abies f. parsonsii is typically used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and essential oils, which can be consumed or applied topically to reap its medicinal benefits.
